description: The Republic of Ireland's Health Service Executive (HSE) has approved ribociclib in combination with an aromatase inhibitor as a treatment option for the adjuvant treatment of patients with hormone receptor positive (HR+), human epidermal growth factor 2 (HER2)-negative early breast cancer at high risk of recurrence. This indication is based on NATALEE (NCT03701334) was a randomized (1:1), open-label, multicenter study, where participants received goserelin and either letrozole or anastrozole, in addition to ribociclib.

contributions.description: Adding HSE approvals for reimbursement to database

contributor.name: Van Allen lab
contributor.description: Van Allen lab, Dana-Farber Cancer Institute

indication.indication: In combination with an aromatase inhibitor (AI) is indicated for the adjuvant treatment of patients with hormone receptor positive (HR+), human epidermal growth factor 2 (HER2)-negative early breast cancer at high risk of recurrence.
indication.description: The Republic of Ireland's Health Service Executive (HSE) has approved ribociclib in combination with an aromatase inhibitor as a treatment option for the adjuvant treatment of patients with hormone receptor positive (HR+), human epidermal growth factor 2 (HER2)-negative early breast cancer at high risk of recurrence. This indication is based on NATALEE (NCT03701334) was a randomized (1:1), open-label, multicenter study, where participants received goserelin and either letrozole or anastrozole, in addition to ribociclib.
